headline: Russia Pounds Ukraine With 189 Drones and 7 Ballistic Missiles as Israel Strikes Hezbollah in Lebanon
subheadline: A MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airbase triggered a nationwide air alert across Ukraine.
lede: Russia launched seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones against Ukrainian industrial facilities overnight, targeting Kremenchuk, Odesa, and Zaporizhzhia, with air defenses intercepting three missiles and 174 drones. In the Middle East, an Israeli airstrike eliminated seven Hezbollah fighters after the group moved weapons to a building near Al-Manazleh in southern Lebanon, while explosions were reported in the Iranian port city of Sirik near the Strait of Hormuz amid continued U.S. strikes against Iranian targets. A U.S.-brokered framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon is expected within hours, even as Lebanese security forces used tear gas to disperse protesters near Beirut's airport.

sections.title: Massive Russian Overnight Barrage
sections.body: Russia launched seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones in a combined overnight attack targeting industrial facilities in Kremenchuk, Odesa region, and Zaporizhzhia, with Ukrainian air defenses intercepting three missiles and 174 drones. A Russian MoD source claimed a strike on an oil refinery in Kremenchuk, Poltava region, causing a partial blackout in the city. A MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, prompting a nationwide air alert due to the weapon's limited interceptability in most regions. Multiple UAV groups were detected across northern, eastern, and southern axes, with Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Odesa, Sumy, Poltava, and Chernihiv regions all under drone threat. Russian ground forces cleared terrain along the railway throughout Krasny Lyman in DNR and advanced in the forest belt southwest of the city. Ukraine conducted a prisoner exchange, releasing 160 military personnel captured in 2022 from sectors including Mariupol and Azovstal.

sections.title: Israeli Strike Kills Seven Hezbollah Fighters
sections.body: Hezbollah transferred weapons to a building in Al-Manazleh, southern Lebanon, used as a combat and observation post, prompting an Israeli airstrike that killed seven militants. Seven Israeli soldiers were injured in a moderate and light manner after an encounter with a militant in southern Lebanon. Iranian state media reported explosions in the port city of Sirik, Hormozgan Province, near the Strait of Hormuz, while the United States announced continued attacks on Iranian regime targets. A U.S.-brokered framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon is expected to be signed within hours, even as Lebanese security forces used tear-gas shells to disperse protesters near the airport.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The parallel escalation in Ukraine and the Middle East signals a period of heightened multi-theater conflict, with Russia demonstrating sustained capacity for large-scale combined ballistic and drone strikes while the U.S. simultaneously conducts strikes against Iran and brokers a Lebanon-Israel framework deal. The MiG-31K Kinzhal scramble and planned Russian escalation to 33,000 FPV drones per day point to a deliberate strategy of attritional pressure on Ukrainian air defenses. In the Middle East, the imminent Israel-Lebanon agreement may stabilize the border but risks provoking deeper internal dissent in Lebanon, as protests near Beirut's airport indicate.
